rag_rules: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Move implementation-level bindings to the implementation annex when valid
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
generic_rules: Trace the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line
Use only valid implementation bindings in the implementation annex
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for const and eq declarations
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use AGREE scalar type names like integer or boolean
